# vgpu - webgpu for agents

VGPU is a WebGPU helper framework for products, visual systems, and coding agents.
It keeps native WebGPU objects visible while providing typed helpers, WGSL utilities,
headless snapshots, and deterministic image artifacts for review.

## Start here

Suggested agent command:

```bash
npx vgpu docs
```

Use the docs command first, then inspect examples and package READMEs before editing GPU code.
Prefer small, typed VGPU helpers when they clarify buffers, textures, layouts, frames,
and snapshots. Keep the native WebGPU escape hatch available for performance-critical paths.

## Site routes

- `/` — VGPU landing page and high-level product story.
- `/preview` — app-local component preview for agents when Storybook is not running.
- `/llms.txt` — this agent guide.

## Repository pointers

- `packages/core` — WebGPU runtime primitives.
- `packages/wgsl` — WGSL resolution and loader utilities.
- `packages/wgsl-std` — reusable WGSL snippets.
- `packages/render/tests/poc` — headless rendering and snapshot examples.
- `apps/vgpu-site/docs/agent-visual-review.md` — visual review rules and artifact commands.

## Agent instructions

1. Run `npx vgpu docs` or read the local package docs before changing shader/render code.
2. Prefer deterministic examples and committed artifacts over one-off screenshots.
3. For UI changes in this site, regenerate `apps/vgpu-site/artifacts/vgpu-site/landing-desktop.png`
   and `apps/vgpu-site/artifacts/vgpu-site/landing-mobile.png`.
4. Keep Agentation as local feedback tooling only; production builds should not depend on it.
